note: Design-stage evals. A full render is a multi-minute GPU+HyperFrames pipeline, so these test the thing the user actually cares about — 'creative per post, no fixed template': given varied posts, the skill must DESIGN structurally-different 9:16 storyboards that each hold the visual genome. Each run produces STORYBOARD.md (1080x1920) + content.json (voiceScript + per-beat scenes) for its post; it does NOT render. Structural diversity is judged ACROSS the three outputs.

evals.name: stat-heavy-post
evals.prompt: Design a hyperframes reel from this post. Do Phase A only (storyboard + voice content.json), no render. Slug: prompt-caching. CAPTION: "Prompt caching cut our token bill by 90%. Same prompts, one config change. Here's what actually happened when we turned it on." SOURCE: Anthropic's prompt-caching docs + our own logs — cached input tokens are billed at 10% of the base rate, cache writes cost 25% more once, and on our agent workload (a ~12k-token system prompt reused across every call) it dropped average cost-per-call from $0.042 to $0.006 and shaved ~1.1s off latency. No image.

evals.name: conceptual-no-numbers
evals.prompt: Design a hyperframes reel from this post. Do Phase A only (storyboard + voice content.json), no render. Slug: context-not-prompts. CAPTION: "Everyone's optimizing prompts. The real unlock is context engineering. Stop tuning wording and start designing what the model can see." SOURCE: Aleem's own take after building agentic systems — the argument is that prompt wording is a local optimum, and the bigger lever is what context (tools, memory, retrieved docs, state) the model has access to at decision time. It's a mindset/positioning piece, no benchmarks, no stats. No image.

evals.name: list-howto-with-image
evals.prompt: Design a hyperframes reel from this post. Do Phase A only (storyboard + voice content.json), no render. Slug: rag-chunking-mistakes. CAPTION: "5 RAG chunking mistakes killing your retrieval quality. #3 is why your AI keeps citing the wrong section." SOURCE: a technical blog roundup — the five: (1) fixed-size chunks that split mid-sentence, (2) no overlap between chunks, (3) ignoring document structure/headings, (4) chunks too large so embeddings blur, (5) no metadata on chunks. The post has an infographic listing all five as a numbered card grid (assume infographic.png is available). Treat it as a listicle.
